preamble.summary: Jacob dies. Joseph carries his body to Canaan. Burial at Machpelah. The sacred books pass to Levi.
preamble.remarkable: Six patriarchs and matriarchs rest in the double cave — the physical anchor of the covenant promise.
preamble.friction: Jubilees abbreviates Jacob's blessing of the twelve tribes almost entirely — possibly because Genesis 49:5-7 curses Simeon and Levi.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob called his sons and blessed them.
verses.rendering: Jacob called his sons and they came to him. He blessed them and told them what would happen in the last days.

verses.text_reference: And he charged them: 'Bury me in the cave of Machpelah.'
verses.rendering: He charged them: Bury me in the cave of Machpelah, in the field Abraham purchased from Ephron the Hittite.

verses.text_reference: And he drew up his feet and died.
verses.rendering: He finished his charge, drew his feet up onto the bed, and died. He was gathered to his fathers.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph wept upon him and kissed him.
verses.rendering: Joseph fell upon his father's face, wept over him, and kissed him.

verses.text_reference: And they embalmed Israel, and mourned seventy days.
verses.rendering: The days of weeping were seventy days. Joseph commanded the physicians to embalm his father.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph went up to bury his father.
verses.rendering: Joseph went up to bury his father in Canaan. All the servants of Pharaoh and the elders of Egypt went with him.

verses.text_reference: And they buried Jacob at Machpelah, beside Abraham, Isaac, Sarah, Rebekah, and Leah.
verses.rendering: They buried Jacob in the double cave, beside Abraham, Isaac, Sarah, Rebekah, and Leah.
translator_notes: Six burials at Machpelah — the most sacred site in the patriarchal tradition.

verses.rendering: Joseph returned to Egypt with his brothers and everyone who had gone up for the burial.

verses.text_reference: And they dwelt in Egypt and kept their father's command.
verses.rendering: Joseph lived in Egypt and all his brothers lived with him. They kept their father's charge.
Judah advised Joseph that each brother should live peacefully with the others. They lived in peace.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob gave all his books to Levi.
verses.rendering: Jacob had given all his books and the books of his fathers to Levi, to preserve and transmit to his descendants.
translator_notes: The chain: Abraham to Isaac to Jacob to Levi.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob was one hundred and forty-seven years old.
verses.rendering: Jacob was one hundred and forty-seven years old — two jubilees, one week, and two years — when he died.

verses.text_reference: And in his life he saw much evil, but from the day he went to Egypt, all was peace.
verses.rendering: In his lifetime he experienced much suffering, but from the day he entered Egypt until his death, all was peace and joy.

verses.text_reference: And he was buried in the double cave.
verses.rendering: He died in Egypt and was buried in the double cave in Canaan.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph ruled in Egypt and was loved by all.
verses.rendering: He gave all his possessions to Joseph. Joseph ruled in Egypt and was loved by all.

verses.text_reference: And they served the Lord all their days.
verses.rendering: Joseph, all his brothers, and everyone who had gone up to bury Jacob returned to Egypt. They served the Lord all their days.
